description: Flutter package scaffolded with standard structure and analysis passing
steps: flutter pub get succeeds in the package directory
flutter analyze reports no issues
Package has lib/, test/, and example/ directories

description: Package is configured to be publishable on pub.dev
steps: flutter pub publish --dry-run completes without errors
Package has a LICENSE file, a README.md, and a CHANGELOG.md
pubspec.yaml has description, homepage or repository, and a valid version

description: Example app demonstrates the package in a running Flutter app
steps: flutter run in example/ builds and launches without errors
Example shows at least one bubble pointing at a target widget
Example source code is minimal and readable as usage documentation

description: Bubble appears at a configurable preferred side relative to the target
steps: When the preferred side is set to top, the bubble appears above the target
When the preferred side is set to bottom, the bubble appears below the target
When set to left or right, the bubble appears on the correct side
An automatic mode picks the side with the most available space

description: Bubble never clips off-screen regardless of where the target sits
steps: A target near the top edge causes the bubble to not clip off the top
A target near a side edge causes the bubble to not clip off that side
The bubble is fully visible for any target position on screen

description: Bubble background can be set to a solid color
steps: Passing a red color renders the bubble with a red background
Changing to a blue color renders a blue background
A sensible default color is used when no color is specified

description: Bubble background can be set to a gradient or color fade
steps: Passing a gradient renders the bubble with that gradient across its surface
A multi-stop color fade is visually continuous across the bubble
Both solid color and gradient are available through the same unified API

description: Bubble text content and style are fully configurable
steps: The bubble renders the text content passed to it
Text color, size, font family, and weight can each be changed independently
Passing a complete custom text style overrides all individual text settings at once

description: Bubble has an optional glow/halo effect
steps: When the bubble halo is enabled, a visible glow surrounds the bubble edges
The halo color and spread can be adjusted
Disabling the halo removes it entirely with no residual visual effect

description: Bubble plays a spring-like scale animation when tapped
steps: Tapping the bubble triggers a visible bouncy scale animation
The bubble returns to its original size after the animation completes
The animation feels elastic or spring-like rather than linear

description: Arrow between bubble and target is optional and can be toggled
steps: When the arrow is enabled, a visible arrow appears connecting bubble and target
When the arrow is disabled, no arrow is rendered and the bubble stands alone
The default state of the arrow is clearly documented

description: Arrow follows a smooth curved path from bubble to target
steps: The arrow path is visibly curved, not a straight line
The curve flows smoothly from the bubble side toward the target
The arrow has a pointed tip or arrowhead at the target end

description: Arrow direction automatically matches the bubble's side relative to the target
steps: When bubble is above the target, the arrow originates from the bubble's bottom and points toward the target's top
When bubble is to the left of the target, the arrow points rightward
Arrow origin and direction update automatically when the bubble side changes

description: Arrow tip lands precisely at the target's border without overlapping it
steps: The arrow tip visually meets the edge of the target widget
The arrow does not extend into or overlap the target's interior
This holds for targets of varying sizes at different positions on screen

description: Arrow has an optional glow/halo effect
steps: When the arrow halo is enabled, a visible glow surrounds the arrow stroke
The arrow halo can be adjusted independently from the bubble halo
Disabling the arrow halo removes it without affecting the arrow itself

description: Overlay darkens everything on screen except the bubble, arrow, and target
steps: When the overlay is active, background UI is visibly dimmed
The target widget appears at full brightness within the overlay
The bubble and arrow are not dimmed
The darkness level of the overlay is configurable

description: Optional glow/halo around the target widget
steps: When the target halo is enabled, a visible glow surrounds the target
Tapping the target still works normally when the halo is active
No halo appears when the option is disabled

description: Any Flutter widget can be targeted without modifying its own code
steps: A standard ElevatedButton is targetable without altering its implementation
A plain Text or Icon widget is targetable the same way
A custom widget with a nested subtree is targetable
The target widget itself requires no changes to participate in the tutorial

description: Tutorial flow can span multiple screens; steps may target widgets on different routes
steps: When a step's target action navigates to a new screen, the tutorial advances and the next step can target a widget on the newly shown screen
The overlay is rendered above the Navigator so it appears over the active route
The bubble and highlight update to the current step's target, which may be on a different screen than the previous step

description: Tapping the target during the tutorial triggers its normal behavior
steps: A button that navigates to another screen still navigates when tapped during the tutorial
A button's action callback fires as expected when tapped during the tutorial
The target behaves identically to how it would with no overlay present

description: All interactions outside the highlighted target are blocked while overlay is active
steps: Tapping any button outside the target during the tutorial produces no action
Scrolling and gestures in non-target areas are intercepted by the overlay
Only the highlighted target remains interactive

description: Tapping the bubble or dark background can optionally advance the tutorial
steps: When this option is enabled, tapping the dark overlay area moves to the next step
When this option is enabled, tapping the bubble also moves to the next step
When this option is disabled, only the target interaction advances the tutorial

description: Bubble and overlay work as a standalone spotlight without any tutorial engine
steps: A single bubble pointing at one target can be shown with no step list and no controller
No engine or sequence setup is required for this single-spotlight usage
The example app demonstrates this standalone mode

description: Engine accepts a list of steps; each step defines a target widget and bubble content
steps: Creating the engine with a list of steps succeeds
Each step specifies which widget to target and what text or content to show in the bubble
The step structure is sufficient for the engine to render the bubble and overlay correctly

description: Engine does not show until explicitly started
steps: Creating the engine with steps does not display any overlay or bubble
Calling start shows the overlay and the first step
The engine remains inactive until start is invoked

description: Engine executes steps in order and advances when the step completion condition is met
steps: Completing a step (target tap or tap-outside when enabled) automatically transitions to the next
Each step shows its bubble on the correct target
Steps run in the order they were defined

description: Tutorial ends and overlay dismisses when the last step completes or when finished early
steps: Completing the final step dismisses the overlay and ends the tutorial
Finishing or skipping the tutorial from any step also dismisses the overlay
After ending, the engine is in a finished state and no overlay remains visible

description: When the current step's target is not yet on screen, the engine handles it gracefully
steps: A step whose target is on a screen not yet pushed does not crash the engine
The overlay appears; the highlight and bubble update when the target becomes available
Or the behavior is clearly documented so consumers order steps to match their navigation flow

description: Tutorial engine reuses the same bubble, arrow, and overlay components as standalone mode
steps: No duplicate implementation of bubble, arrow, or overlay for the engine
The engine composes the same visual components used in standalone spotlight mode
Changes to bubble styling apply to both engine and standalone usage

description: Visual parameters have global defaults and can be overridden per step
steps: A global bubble color applies to all steps unless a step defines its own
A step with its own bubble color uses that color instead of the global one
Arrow visibility, halo settings, overlay opacity, and text style follow the same global-then-step-override pattern

description: Tutorial engine persists progress and resumes from where it left off across app restarts
steps: Completing three steps, closing the app, and reopening resumes from step four
Resetting the saved state causes the tutorial to restart from the first step
Persistence requires no additional configuration from the package consumer

description: Progress is only saved at configurable checkpoint steps
steps: With checkpoints at steps 2 and 5, only reaching those steps updates the saved position
Completing a non-checkpoint step does not advance the persisted position
Saving at every step, saving only at checkpoints, and never saving are all valid configurations

description: Controller provides programmatic control: advance, skip, finish
steps: The controller can advance to the next step from outside the tutorial UI
The controller can skip the current step without triggering the target's action
The controller can end the tutorial entirely from any step
All three actions work when invoked programmatically (e.g. from a button or lifecycle)

description: Controller exposes state and notifies when the active step changes
steps: The controller exposes the current step index and total step count as readable values
A listener or stream fires whenever the active step changes
The app can react to step changes (e.g. enable/disable a Next button)

description: Controller allows registering a callback when the tutorial completes
steps: The app can register an on-complete callback when creating or configuring the engine
The callback runs when the tutorial ends (last step completed, or finished/skipped early)
The callback receives enough context to know how the tutorial ended (completed vs skipped vs finished)

description: Controller can navigate to the previous step
steps: The controller provides a way to go back to the previous step
Going back from step 2 shows step 1 again with its target and bubble
Going back from the first step either does nothing or is undefined and documented
